title: Numeros 11–100
summary: Count past ten, and use ordinals.

teach.note: Ordinals: prime, secunde, tertie… mostly add -e to the number stem.

teach.target: Io es le prime in mi classe.
teach.roman: io es le prime in mi classe
teach.english: I am first in my class.

grammar.title: Numbers past ten
grammar.body:
Interlingua counts like a Romance language: **dece-un** (11), **dece-duo** (12), up to **vinti** (20). Bigger numbers keep composing: **vinti-un** (21), **trenta** (30), **quaranta** (40), **cinquanta** (50), and so on to **cento** (100).
Ordinals mostly just add **-e**: prime (1st), secunde (2nd), tertie (3rd), quarte (4th).

teach.target: Nos ha trenta studentes in le schola.
teach.roman: nos ha trenta studentes in le schola
teach.english: We have thirty students in the school.
